revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:彦人大兄命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Keikō|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}|mother={{ill|Q108044550|qid=Q108044550|1=Inabiwakarōjo}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}; {{ill|Prince Iokiirihiko|qid=Q11372557|1=Prince Iokiirihiko}}; {{ill|Prince Kamukushi|qid=Q11590432|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Oousu|qid=Q11438297|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Takekunikoriwake|qid=Q11545861|1=Prince Takekunikoriwake}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}}}
'''Hikohito Ōe no Mikoto''' (彦人大兄命) was a member of the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] during the [[Kofun period]].
The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' refers to him simply as '''Hikohito Ōe''' (without honorific titles) and states that he was an uncle of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, who took Hikohito Ōe’s daughter, Ōnakahime, as a consort. In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he is listed as '''Hikohito Ōe no Ō''' and described as a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} and {{ill|Inabino Wakairatsume|ja|伊那毘若郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabino Wakairatsume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Wakatake Kibi no Mikoto|ja|若建吉備津日子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatake Kibi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hikohito Ōe no Ō had a full brother, {{ill|Manowaka no Ō|ja|真若王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manowaka no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but no records of wives or children are provided.
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', another individual named '''Ōe no Ō''' (also spelled '''Ōeda no Ō''') appears, whose mother is {{ill|Kagurohime|ja|迦具漏比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagurohime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Kagurohime is described as the great-granddaughter of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}, making Emperor Keikō's union with her an unlikely yet theoretically possible case of direct-line intermarriage if Emperor Keikō lived to an extraordinary age. Some researchers, however, view this genealogy as erroneous and suggest that Hikohito Ōe no Mikoto and Ōe no Ō might be the same person.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=喜一郎 大久間|date=1993-03-01|url=https://hdl.handle.net/10291/10103|title=景行天皇記に於ける倭建命|journal=明治大学教養論集|volume=259|pages=1-12|issn=03896005|publisher=明治大学教養論集刊行会}}</ref>
The ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' also mentions another individual named Kagurohime (possibly the same person) as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, but the list of children attributed to her differs. Similarly, Ōe no Ō is said to have married his half-sister, Ginganohime (銀王), with whom he had two children: Daiminakata no Ō (大名方王) and Ōnakahime (大中比売命), the latter becoming the consort of Emperor Chūai. This overlaps with the records of Hikohito Ōe no Mikoto’s offspring.
A separate individual, {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko|ja|押坂彦人大兄皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko|1=Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11497661}}, from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} shares a similar name but is a distinct person.
== References == <references />
